# APT packages installed in piano-base.
# One package per line. Lines starting with # are ignored.
#
# Piano-base is self-contained: everything a machine needs is here.
# User installs extras via `sudo apt install` (persists in overlay upper).

# Shell — zsh is the default and only shell piano uses.
# bash kept as fallback (some scripts assume /bin/bash).
zsh
bash

# Basic editors
# neovim installed via mise (see Containerfile.machine) — apt's version is too old (vim.uv missing).
nano

# Network fundamentals
curl
wget
ca-certificates
iproute2
iptables

# Dev essentials
git

# sudo — exec sessions run as the host user (--user uid:gid); sudo bridges to container root for package installs and podman operations.
# NOPASSWD configured in Containerfile.machine.
sudo

# Podman-in-Podman (nested containers inside machines).
# Runs rootful via sudo wrapper — no nested user namespace issues.
podman
crun
slirp4netns
uidmap
fuse-overlayfs

# BTRFS tools — inner daemon needs mkfs.btrfs to create its own BTRFS sparse image
# for O(1) subvolume snapshots (recursive machine nesting).
btrfs-progs

# SFTP server — used by SSH Gateway for IDE file editing (VS Code / Windsurf / Cursor).
# Part of openssh-sftp-server, no full sshd needed.
openssh-sftp-server

# socat — bidirectional TCP relay, used by SSH Gateway for port forwarding
# into localhost-bound services inside containers.
socat

# FUSE client — AppImages, sshfs, archivemount, etc.
# Pairs with /dev/fuse device passthrough in ContainerOpts.
fuse3
